chinese_text: 入國而不存其士則亡國矣見賢而不急則緩其君矣
translation_text: Formerly Lord Wen (of Chin 780-746 b.c.) was once in exile and yet later became the leading feudal lord. Lord Huan (of Chh 685-643 b.c.) was once forced to leave his state and yet later became a tyrant " among the feudal lords. Lord Kou Chien of Yiieh (496-465 b.c.) was once brought under humiliation by the king of Wu and yet he was later looked upon with awe by the princes of CHna. Thus, Pi Kan died of his up-rightness; Meng Pen perished by his strength; Hsi Shih paid with her life for her beauty; and Wu Ch"i was tom alive for his achievement. The water in a river does not come from a single source, neither is the fur coat that is worth a thousand yi:coBiposeci: of tte white fmr of a single fox.

chinese_text: 齊桓染於管仲、鮑叔，晉文染於舅犯、高偃，楚莊染於孫叔、沈尹，吳闔閭染於伍員、文義，越句踐染於范蠡大夫種。此五君者所染當，故霸諸侯，功名傳於後世。范吉射染於長柳朔、王胜，中行寅染於籍秦、高彊，吳夫差染於王孫雒、太宰嚭，智伯搖染於智國、張武，中山尚染於魏義、偃長，宋康染於唐鞅、佃不禮。
translation_text: Lord Huan of Ch’i came under the influence of Kuan Chung and Pao Shu; Lord Wen of Chin, under that of Uncle Fan 2 and Kao Yen 2; Lord Chuang of Ch’u, under that of Sun Shu and the Minister of Shen; Ho Lu of Wu, under that of Wu Yuan and Wen Yi; and Kou Chien of Yueh, under that of Pan Li and Minister Chimg. Now these five lords had been under good influences. Therefore they became Tyrants among the feudal lords and their achievements and their fame were handed down to posterity. Fan Chi She came under the influence of Ch’ang Liu Shuo and Wang Sheng «; Chung Hsing Yin, under that of Chi Ch’in and Kao Chiang Pu Ch’a. under that of Wang Sun Lo and Minister P’i «; Chih Po Yao, under that of These are the noted wicked men in ancient Chinese historj-, assistants ° accomplices with them in sin and cruelty. These are the able assistants to the great feudal lords of the period WHO are mstrumental to the latter’s success.

chinese_text: 凡君之所以安者，何也？以其行理也，行理性於染當。故善為君者，勞於論人，而佚於治官。不能為君者，傷形費神，愁心勞意，然國逾危，身逾辱。
translation_text: Therefore their states were ruined and they were executed, their ancestral temples were destroyed and descendants annihilated. The rulers and the subjects were dispersed and the people were left homeless. The whole world point to these six princes as the most greedy and disturbing people. Now, how’ can the rulers obtain security? They can obtain it by following the right way. And one will naturally follow the right way when under good influence.

chinese_text: 此六君者，非不重其國，愛其身也，以不知要故也。不知要者，所染不當也。非獨國有染也，士亦有染。其友皆好仁義，淳謹畏令，則家日益，身日安，名日榮，處官得其理矣，則段干木、禽子、傅說之徒是也。
translation_text: Therefore capable rulers are very painstaking in the selection of men while they may not be so careful in attending to the administration (themselves). But the incapable wear out their body and exhaust their energy, tax their mind and stretch their thought, and yet their states are only placed in greater danger and their persons under more humiliation. Now, it is not that these six princes do not value their states or hold their lives cheap, it is really that they do not understand the relative importance of things. And it is due to bad influences that their idea of importance is distorted. Not only states but also individuals are subject to influences. If one has for friends none but those who love magnanimity and righteousness and who are careful and respectful of course one’s family will become more prosperous, one’s person more at peace, and one’s name more honourable every day; and, as an official, one will be properly qualified.
